briefs.title: BC Removes Ministerial Approval Requirement for Housing Density Zoning Bylaws Near Highways
briefs.summary: BC updated its rules so that local governments do not need provincial minister approval before passing certain zoning bylaws (local rules about how land can be used) that allow small-scale multi-family housing near controlled access highways (major roads where entry and exit points are limited). This exemption applies only to bylaws made to meet housing needs report requirements and expires January 1, 2026. Bylaws that affect direct access to a controlled access highway still require approval.
briefs.action_required: If your business involves land near a major BC highway, check whether upcoming rezoning plans are affected before January 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC housing needs report zoning approval exemption expires — Transportation Act

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
